parts.description: large red ladder/frame-style subassembly with long vertical members and diagonal/connected cross features
green small bracket/link attached near upper/right region of step-0 assembly
purple small bracket/link attached near lower/right region of step-0 assembly
large red upright frame assembly with two tall rails and multiple cross/link features
large red upright frame assembly variant with added central/front vertical rod and distributed connectors
green long rod/rail component mounted on left/front side of step-2 assembly
purple long rod/rail component mounted on right/front side of step-2 assembly
large red multi-upright frame assembly with several long vertical rods and lower attachments
large red final main body assembly with several tall posts/rails and top linkage details
green base/platform assembly with many slats/braces and side supports

step_analysis.analysis: One major red subassembly is present. Two smaller differently colored parts (green and purple) are shown being attached to this red structure at different right-side/lower-right connection points. Result is a 3-part subassembly.
Single-color step, so this is one independent part/subassembly. It appears to be a large upright frame module. No additional independent colored parts are introduced in this step.

A large red upright frame/subassembly is combined with two long auxiliary rails/rods (green and purple), one on each side. This creates a more complete vertical module. Geometry suggests step-1 and step-2 are related variants/evolution of the same overall side/upright assembly, but because step-2 contains explicit additional independent colored parts, it is treated as a new assembly event: red main body plus two side rods.

Single-color step again, representing one larger subassembly/black-box module with multiple uprights and linkages. Likely a later-stage integrated body derived from earlier frame work, but only one independent part is visible in this step.

Single-color step, showing an even more complete main body/frame with several tall posts, cross-link features, and upper attachments. This is best treated as the evolved main product body/subassembly prior to joining with the base.

Single-color step showing a large green base/platform/chassis-like structure with many braces and slats. As this is the final step and only one color is visible, the previously built upper/main-body subassembly is likely reused as a black-box or omitted from exploded visibility conventions; conservatively, this step contributes the remaining major base subassembly to form the final product.
